---
title: "Computational Musicology"
format: 
  dashboard:
    orientation: columns
    theme:
      - flatly
      - custom.scss
    toc: false
---

# Weekly Assignments {width=50%}

## Course Assignments

::: {.card}
### Week 7: Computational Musicology and (Audio) Visualisation
(not available right now, will be added later)
:::

::: {.card}
### Week 8: Chroma Features
(not available right now, will be added later)
:::

::: {.card}
### Week 9: Timbre Features / Self-Similarity Matrices
(not available right now, will be added later)
:::

::: {.card}
### Week 10: Key and Chord Estimation
(not available right now, will be added later)
:::

::: {.card}
### Week 11: Novelty Functions and Tempograms
Analysis of *Afraid to Feel* by LF System

[Open page](week11.html)
:::

::: {.card}
### Week 12: Classification and Clustering
Clustering my Spotify 2025 Wrapped

[Open page](week12.html)
:::

# Portfolio {width=50%}

## Portfolio sections

::: {.card}
### Background
Motivation, corpus design, and portfolio structure.

[Open page](portfolio/background.html)
:::

::: {.card}
### Corpus Exploration
A first general inspection of the corpus using Spotify track-level features.

[Open page](portfolio/corpus_exploration.html)
:::

::: {.card}
### Feature Analysis
Chroma, loudness, timbre, and temporal features.

[Open page](portfolio/feature_analysis.html)
:::

::: {.card}
### Classification and Clustering
A broader corpus-level analysis.

[Open page](portfolio/classification_clustering.html)  

:::


::: {.card}
### Conclusion
What have we learned from this set of analyses? 

[Open page](conclusion.html)
:::
